name: qsv-pragmastat
description: Compute pragmatic statistics using the Pragmastat library. Uses the stats cache to auto-filter non-numeric columns and support Date/DateTime columns.

options.description: One-sample confirmatory analysis. Test center/spread against thresholds. Format: metric:value[,metric:value,...]. Mutually exclusive with --twosample and --compare2.
Two-sample confirmatory analysis. Test shift/ratio/disparity against thresholds. Format: metric:value[,metric:value,...]. Mutually exclusive with --twosample and --compare1.
The field delimiter for reading/writing CSV data. Must be a single character. (default: ,)
Force recomputing ps_* columns even if they already exist in the stats cache.
Check if there is enough memory to load the entire CSV into memory using CONSERVATIVE heuristics. Not valid for stdin.
Probability that bounds fail to contain the true parameter. Lower values produce wider bounds. Must be achievable for the given sample size.
Skip confidence bounds computation (~2x faster). Incompatible with --compare1/--compare2.
When set, the first row will not be treated as headers.
Write output to <file> instead of stdout.
Round statistics to <n> decimal places. Rounding follows Midpoint Nearest Even (Bankers Rounding) rule.
Seed for reproducible subsampling. If not specified, defaults to 42 when --subsample is used.
Select columns for analysis. Uses qsv's column selection syntax. Non-numeric columns appear with n=0. In two-sample mode, all pairs of selected columns are computed.
Output one-sample results as standalone CSV instead of appending to the stats cache.
Options to pass to the stats command if baseline stats need to be generated. The options are passed as a single string that will be split by whitespace.
Randomly subsample N values per column before computing. Speeds up large datasets while maintaining statistical robustness. Recommended: 10000-50000 for exploratory analysis.
Compute two-sample estimators for all column pairs.

examples.description: Append pragmastat columns to stats cache (default one-sample behavior)
One-sample statistics with very tight bounds (lower misrate)
Compare one-sample center against a threshold
Compare one-sample center and spread against thresholds
Compare two-sample shift and disparity against thresholds
Fast exploratory analysis with subsampling (~100x speedup on large datasets)
Reproducible subsampling with a specific seed
